seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building a RAG pipeline with Pydantic AI enables type-safe, structured document retrieval and synthesis for production applications. Guardrails are essential to mitigate risks like hallucination, source infidelity, and citation errors that can undermine reliability in high-stakes retrieval tasks. Vex integration provides automated verification for source faithfulness and context relevance, ensuring trustworthy outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
The RAG pipeline begins with vector search over a document corpus using embeddings to retrieve relevant chunks. Retrieved contexts are assembled and injected into a Pydantic AI agent prompt, which uses structured tools for synthesis and citation generation. The agent produces a typed response with source attribution, validated against schema constraints.

Pydantic AI's agent definitions enforce output structure via Pydantic models, while tools handle retrieval and formatting. Vex result validators run post-generation to check hallucination, faithfulness, and citation accuracy beyond syntactic validation.

This architecture supports streaming but requires careful validation to handle partial outputs and nested tool calls.

risks.name: Source Faithfulness Failure
risks.description: LLM generates claims not grounded in retrieved contexts despite valid structure
risks.mitigation: Vex faithfulness checks trace every claim to source chunks

risks.name: Hallucination in Synthesis
risks.description: Agent fabricates details when context is ambiguous or incomplete
risks.mitigation: Semantic similarity scoring between output and context

risks.name: Citation Drift
risks.description: Incorrect or missing source attribution in structured citations
risks.mitigation: Vex citation validation matching references to actual context usage

risks.name: Context Relevance Gap
risks.description: Retrieved chunks don't actually answer the query despite vector similarity

implementation.title: Vector Search Tool Definition
implementation.description: Pydantic AI tool for semantic document retrieval using embeddings
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Tool, Agent
from typing import List
import chromadb

class RetrieveDocuments(Tool):
    name = 'retrieve_documents'
    description = 'Retrieve relevant documents for RAG'

    def __call__(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> List[str]:
        client = chromadb.Client()
        collection = client.get_collection('docs')
        results = collection.query(
            query_embeddings=[self.embed(query)],
            n_results=top_k
        )
        return results['documents'][0]

    def embed(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
        # Use your embedding model
        from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
        model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
        return model.encode(text).tolist()

implementation.title: RAG Agent with Structured Output
implementation.description: Main agent definition with Pydantic response model and citation tracking
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Annotated
from pydantic_ai import Agent

class Citation(BaseModel):
    chunk_id: int
    start_char: int
    end_char: int
    source_text: str

class RAGResponse(BaseModel):
    answer: str = Field(..., max_length=2000)
    citations: List[Citation] = Field(..., min_items=1)
    confidence: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)

rag_agent = Agent(
    'rag-synthesis',
    model='gpt-4o',
    response_model=RAGResponse,
    tools=[RetrieveDocuments()]
)

implementation.title: Context Assembly and Prompting
implementation.description: Assemble retrieved contexts with citation metadata for the agent

implementation.title: Streaming RAG Response
implementation.description: Handle streaming structured outputs with progressive validation
implementation.code:
async def stream_rag_response(query: str):
    stream = rag_agent.stream(
        assemble_context(query, retrieved_docs),
        stream_mode='structured'
    )
    
    async for chunk in stream:
        if hasattr(chunk, 'content'):
            print(chunk.content, end='', flush=True)
        # Vex validates complete structured response
        if chunk.done:
            yield chunk.response_model

# Usage
async for response in stream_rag_response(query):
    print(f'Confidence: {response.confidence}')
